originalTitle: Kidnapped Nigerian retired general dies in captivity
title: Learning About Safety: A Story from Nigeria
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your friendly reporter, Fara from Africa, here to share some news with you. News can sometimes be happy, and sometimes it can make us feel a little bit sad. Today, we have some news from a big and beautiful country called Nigeria.

A Special Helper Named General Abubakar

In every country, we have special people who help keep everyone safe. These people work very hard every day. In Nigeria, there was a very special man named General Abubakar. He was like a superhero, but a real-life one! He worked in the army. The army is a big group of brave people who protect their country and all the people in it. They wear special uniforms and train to be strong and helpful. General Abubakar was a "general." That means he was a very, very important leader in the army. He helped make big plans to keep everyone away from harm. He spent many, many years helping Nigeria. Even after he stopped working, which we say he "retired," he was still a very respected and loved person in his community. He loved his country very much and always tried his best to help others. Did you know? Nigeria is a country in West Africa. It has so many different kinds of people and beautiful places! It's one of the biggest countries in Africa!

What Happened to General Abubakar?

Sometimes, in the world, there are people who do bad things. This makes everyone feel sad and worried. One day, General Abubakar was taken away from his home. This is called "being kidnapped." It means someone takes a person away when they don't want to go. This is a very wrong thing to do, and it made many people in Nigeria very, very sad. Everyone wished he could come back home safely. We all hoped and prayed for his safe return.

Sad News for Everyone

We waited and hoped, but sadly, General Abubakar is no longer with us. He passed away while he was taken away. This is very, very sad news for his family, his friends, and all the people in Nigeria who loved and respected him. When we hear sad news like this, it's okay to feel sad. It's important to remember all the good things General Abubakar did to help everyone. He was a brave and kind man who dedicated his life to serving his country and its people.

Why Do Bad Things Happen?

Sometimes, it's hard to understand why bad things happen. In some parts of Nigeria, just like in other parts of the world, there are challenges. These "challenges" mean that it's sometimes hard to keep everyone safe all the time. There might be some people who cause trouble. But remember, for every person who causes trouble, there are many, many more good people trying to help. These good people work every day to make things better and safer for everyone. They are like shining lights in the dark. Did you know? There are over 250 different groups of people living in Nigeria, all speaking different languages! They are all part of one big Nigerian family, working and living together.

Who Helps Us Stay Safe?

Even when things are challenging, there are always brave and kind people working hard to protect us. Let's think about who these amazing helpers are: * **The Police:** They are like our everyday heroes. They wear uniforms and drive special cars. They help people when they are in trouble and try to stop bad things from happening in our towns and cities. * **The Army:** Like General Abubakar, the army works very hard to protect the country from big dangers. They train to be strong and brave and keep our borders safe. * **Community Leaders:** These are important grown-ups in our towns and villages. They help make rules and plans to keep everyone living happily together. They listen to people and try to solve problems peacefully. * **Our Parents and Teachers:** They are our first and most important protectors! They teach us how to be safe, how to make good choices, and how to tell a grown-up if something feels wrong or scary. They are always there for us.

Working Together for a Safer Future

When something sad happens, it makes everyone think about how important it is to be safe. All the good people in Nigeria, and all over Africa, want everyone to be safe and happy. They are working together. The government, the police, the army, and everyday people are trying their very best to make sure these sad things don't happen again. They are talking, planning, and helping each other to build a better and safer future for all children. Did you know? Africa is a giant continent with 54 amazing countries! Each one is special and full of wonderful people and places.

What Can We Do?

Even as kids, we can help make our communities safer! Every little bit helps to make the world a better place: * **Be kind:** When we are kind to each other, our communities become happier places. Kindness makes everyone feel good and helps us become friends. * **Listen to grown-ups:** Always listen to your parents, teachers, and other trusted adults. They tell you important things to keep you safe and healthy. * **Tell someone:** If you ever see something that makes you feel worried or unsafe, tell a trusted grown-up right away. Don't keep it a secret. Talking helps solve problems. * **Be a good friend:** Look out for your friends. Help them if they need it. Share your toys and your smiles. Good friends make each other feel safe and happy.

Hopes for Peace and Safety

General Abubakar's story reminds us that safety is very important. Everyone in Nigeria, and all around the world, deserves to feel safe and happy in their homes and communities. We can all hope for a future where there is more peace, more kindness, and fewer sad stories. The people of Nigeria are strong, and they will keep working for a bright and safe future for all their children. They have big hearts and strong spirits. We believe in them! Did you know? The baobab tree, often called the "tree of life," grows in many parts of Africa and can live for thousands of years! It's a symbol of strength and endurance.

Remembering General Abubakar

We remember General Abubakar as a brave man who helped his country. His life reminds us to appreciate all the people who work to keep us safe every day. Let's all do our part, no matter how small, to make our homes, schools, and communities wonderful and safe places for everyone to grow and play. Thank you for listening, my friends. Let's always remember to be kind and look out for each other! Sending big hugs from Africa!
summary: A special army leader from Nigeria, General Abubakar, sadly passed away after being taken away, reminding us how important it is for everyone to work together to keep our communities safe and happy.
whyMatters: This story helps us remember that feeling safe is very important for everyone, and many brave people, like the police and army, work hard every day to protect us all.

originalTitle: Ghana government wants review of Partey Canada ban
title: Ghana's Hero Thomas Partey Needs Our Cheers!
content:
Hello, super friends! Welcome to our special news for kids from Africa! We have some exciting news about a very special football player from Ghana. His name is Thomas Partey! He plays football for our country, Ghana. He is a big star on the field!

Who is Thomas Partey?

Thomas Partey is a strong and fast player. He plays in the middle of the football field. This important job is called a "midfielder." He helps his team move the ball forward to score goals. He also helps stop the other team from scoring. He is like a superhero for Ghana's football team! Thomas Partey works very hard. He practices a lot to be the best he can be. He makes all of us in Ghana very proud when he plays. He is a true champion for our country. Did you know? Thomas Partey also plays for a very big and famous football team in a country far away called England! That team is called Arsenal!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine a super big football party! That's what the World Cup is! It's the biggest football competition in the whole world. Teams from many, many countries come together to play. They want to find out which country has the best football team on Earth. The World Cup is a very special event. It happens only every four years! It's a time when everyone watches football and cheers for their country. It makes people feel happy and united. It is a very exciting time for football fans everywhere! Did you know? The World Cup is so big that billions of people watch it on TV! That's more people than live in many countries!

Ghana's Black Stars!

Our Ghana football team has a very cool name! They are called the Black Stars. Our Black Stars wear beautiful football jerseys with our Ghana colours. They run and kick and play with all their heart for our country. They want to make Ghana proud. Thomas Partey is a very, very important part of our Black Stars team. He is one of their best players. When he plays, the team feels stronger and has more chances to win. He helps lead them on the field. Did you know? The star on Ghana's flag is also a black star, just like our football team's name!

A Little Problem for Thomas

Now, for the news about Thomas. Thomas Partey had a little problem. He was told he could not go to a country called Canada. This is what "banned" means – it means he cannot enter that country right now. Ghana was going to play a very important game in Canada soon. It was a surprise for everyone! The team needs Thomas Partey to play. He is a very important player for them. Not having him there would make the team sad and maybe a little weaker for the game. Did you know? Canada is a very big country far away from Ghana, on another continent! It can get very cold there, with lots of snow!

Why Can't He Go?

Sometimes, grown-ups make rules about who can visit their country. We don't know all the reasons why Thomas was told he could not go to Canada. It's a bit like when you have rules at home or at school, and sometimes you can't do certain things because of those rules. These rules are usually for a good reason, but sometimes they can cause problems, especially when someone important like Thomas Partey wants to play for his country. Everyone in Ghana wants to see him play!

Ghana's Government Steps In!

Our country has a "government." The government is like a big team of grown-ups who help run Ghana. They make sure things are good for everyone in our country. They help build schools, hospitals, and roads. They also help important people like our football stars! Our government heard about Thomas Partey's problem. They want to help him! They want to "review" his ban. This means they are asking Canada to please look again at the rules. They want Canada to please let Thomas Partey go and play with his team! They are sending messages and talking to the people in Canada. They are working very hard for Thomas Partey and for all of Ghana. They know how important he is to our team and our country's happiness. Did you know? Governments represent all the people in a country, like a big spokesperson for everyone!

Why Is This Important for Ghana?

Thomas Partey is a very, very good player. His team needs him to be strong and to play their best. When our Black Stars play well and win games, it makes everyone in Ghana feel so proud and happy! It makes us all cheer together. It's important for our team to have all their best players, especially for big games. When Thomas Partey plays, he inspires other young boys and girls in Ghana to play football too! He shows them that they can achieve their dreams. Our government wants to show the world that Ghana cares for its heroes. They want to make sure Thomas Partey can play for his country and make us all smile. Did you know? When you cheer for your team, it helps them feel strong and play even better! Your cheers are like magic energy!

Hope for Thomas!

So, our government is working very hard to help Thomas Partey. We hope Canada will say "yes" and let him go to play with his team. Let's all cheer loudly for Thomas Partey and our amazing Black Stars! We want them to shine bright like the stars they are! Keep your fingers crossed, super friends, and let's believe that Thomas Partey will be on the field, making us all proud! Go Black Stars, go!
summary: Ghana's government is asking Canada to let star football player Thomas Partey enter the country so he can play for the national team.
whyMatters: This is important because Thomas Partey is a very good player for Ghana's football team, and his team needs him to play their best and make our country proud.

title: Was this offside? Fifa under pressure to release VAR images

originalTitle: Saibari's 'clinical' finish gives Morocco lead against Brazil
title: Saibari's 'clinical' finish gives Morocco lead against Brazil
content: Morocco's Ismael Saibari fires his side into the lead against Brazil with a clinical finish in the 21st minute of their Group C match at the 2026 World Cup.
